questions.question: According to the 80/20 Community Rule, what percentage of your interactions should be dedicated to 'Pure Value Contribution'?
questions.explanation: To build a sustainable trust-led engine, you must prioritize contribution. 80% contribution creates the 'Trust Balance' needed to effectively promote your work later.

questions.question: What is the key difference between 'Selfish' and 'Selfless' self-promotion?

options.text: Selfish promotion is cheaper to run.
Selfish promotion says 'I made this!', while selfless promotion says 'I made this FOR YOU' to solve a specific community pain.
Selfless promotion never includes a link to your product.
Selfish promotion is done by brands; selfless promotion is done by influencers.

questions.explanation: Framing matters. When you position your product as a solution to a problem the community has already expressed, it feels like a contribution rather than an advertisement.

questions.question: In the 'Trust Bank' analogy, what happens if you try to make a 'Withdrawal' (Promotion) with an empty balance?

options.text: The community will give you a loan of trust.
Your post will be 'Declined' (ignored, flagged as spam, or leading to a ban).
LinkedIn will automatically increase your reach.
You will be asked to pay a fine to the moderators.

questions.explanation: Without a history of helpfulness, a promotional post is seen as 'Value Extraction.' Communities protect themselves from this by ignoring or removing the content.

questions.question: Which of these is considered a 'High-Value Deposit' into a community trust bank?

options.text: Posting a link to your blog every day.
Answering a technical question thoroughly without any self-promotional links.
Liking every post in the newsfeed without commenting.
Messaging members privately to ask for a meeting.

questions.explanation: Selfless contribution is the most powerful way to build authority. Answering a question well proves you are an expert and a helper.

questions.question: Why is 'Consistency' recommended over 'Intensity' for community marketing?

options.text: Because people recognize and trust familiar names. 5 minutes a day builds long-term reputation better than a massive blast once a month.
Because you get more points for daily logins.
It isn't; you should aim for one viral post per month.

questions.explanation: Community value is human-to-human. Being a recurring 'helpful neighbor' is more effective than being an 'intermittent advertiser.'

questions.question: Look back at your last 5 interactions in your primary community. If you were to calculate your current 'Trust Balance', what would the ratio be (Contribution % vs. Promotion %)? Based on today's lesson, what is one 'Selfless' resource you could create and share in the next 7 days that would move the needle for your ICP?
promptHints: Be honest about your current 'Pitch vs. Help' ratio.
Think of a problem you see members asking about repeatedly.
Consider a template or checklist you already use internally.
